subject.title: Sports, exercise and health science (SEHS - Old)

strengths: Clear articulation of personal interest and motivation in exercise science
Independent design and management of data collection, demonstrating initiative
Real-world relevance by aiming to provide practical tools for peers and coaches
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student exhibits significant personal engagement throughout: choosing a topic rooted in their own sporting interests, organising participants, designing warm-up protocols and leading sessions. The write-up consistently reflects independent thinking and real-world significance, justifying the full 2 marks.

strengths: Thorough safety and ethical considerations (PAR-Q, consent forms, injury prevention)
Relevant and largely focused research question specifying population, activity and measurement
Systematic and replicable methodology with detailed procedures and controls
weaknesses: Background review relies on non-peer-reviewed sources and lacks depth in academic rigor
Methodology does not include randomisation or counter-balancing to mitigate order effects
Minor imprecision in research question wording and hypothesis formulation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration demonstrates strong ethical awareness and a procedure that is largely appropriate and replicable. The focused research question and detailed controls place this work near the top band, but shortcomings in academic sourcing and experimental design justify a score of 5/6.

strengths: Sufficient and well-presented raw data across multiple participants and trials
Accurate and appropriate use of statistical processing (means, SD, CV, t-tests)
Correct and meaningful interpretation linking statistical results to biomechanical reasoning
weaknesses: Consideration of measurement uncertainty is limited to instrument resolution
Overstatement of practical significance when p-values exceed 0.05
Minor formatting and rounding inconsistencies in tables and graphs
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis includes rich data and largely accurate processing, with valid interpretations grounded in statistics and theory. A fuller treatment of uncertainty and more cautious interpretation would be needed for the top band, placing this work solidly at 5/6.

strengths: Relevant conclusion supported by mean results and significance tests
Identification of key methodological limitations showing some awareness
Integration of literature context, including meta-analyses on plyometrics
weaknesses: Suggestions for improvement are few and lack detailed procedural recommendations
Discussion of limitations omits order effects and sample size considerations
Scientific comparison lacks depth and critical evaluation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation provides a supported conclusion and acknowledges limitations, with some reference to scientific context. However, the limited scope and detail of improvement suggestions and methodological critique justify a mid-band score of 4/6.

strengths: Clear structure with logical IA format and well-labelled sections
Appropriate use of subject terminology with minimal errors
Generally clear tables, figures and headings aiding comprehension
weaknesses: Occasional formatting glitches, typos and inconsistent table/figure labels
Grammar and style errors in conclusion and bibliography reduce professionalism
Some extraneous narrative and inconsistent presentation details hinder conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report is well organised and employs correct terminology, facilitating understanding. Minor presentation errors and inconsistent formatting prevent top marks, but the overall clarity and coherence merit 4/4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Uncertainty is only acknowledged as Â±0.05 cm for measurement; there is no propagation or discussion of trial variability beyond SD/CV. Limited treatment places the work in the âsome considerationâ category.
The discussion cites meta-analyses on plyometrics and literature on dynamic warm-ups, offering modest integration with scientific context. Depth and critical comparison remain limited.
The report follows a logical IA structure (Introduction-Method-Analysis-Evaluation-Conclusion) with section titles and numbered steps. All required information is present and easy to locate.
The student suggests recruiting more similar-ability participants and extending duration, which are realistic but few and not elaborated into detailed procedural improvements, fitting the lower band.

The research question is relevant and largely focused, specifying population, activities, outcome and measurement precision. Minor wording issues (âgirls vertical jumpâ vs. âgirlsââ) slightly reduce precision, but overall focus is maintained across the report.

Limitations such as participant maturity, measurement error and uncontrolled motivation are identified with some explanation. Methodological awareness is evident but not fully developed (e.g., no mention of order effects or sample size power).

The procedure is systematic and replicable, listing equipment, warm-up, activity scripts and measurement steps. Nevertheless, measurement technique (wall-mark method) and control of fatigue/learning effects are insufficiently justified, and independent-variable control relies heavily on helpers, so the design is âmainly appropriate with limitationsâ.

Key muscular, physiological and literature-based context is provided with citations; however, depth is uneven and some sources (YouTube, blog) are of limited academic quality. Explanations of biomechanics are mostly accurate but not exhaustive.
Most content is relevant to answering the research question, though some narrative repetition and extraneous quotations reduce conciseness. Overall focus remains adequate.

Interpretation links mean values, SD overlap and t-test significance to hypotheses and provides biomechanical reasoning. However, the student overstates practical significance when most p-values are >0.05, so conclusions are âbroadly valid but incompleteâ.
Tables, figures and headings make the report generally clear. Occasional formatting glitches (broken table borders, typos) slightly hinder readability but not comprehension.

The student clearly articulates a personal interest in exercise science and explains how online research and club trials inspired the choice of topic. The student personally organised participants, designed multiple warm-up protocols, and managed data collection. References to âmy programmeâ, trial runs at the local hockey club, and leading the sessions all demonstrate substantial independent thinking and ownership.

Means, standard deviations, coefficient of variation and paired t-tests are correctly calculated (software used) and presented in appropriately labelled tables and a graph with error bars. Minor rounding/label issues are present but do not undermine overall accuracy.

The introduction links the investigation to the studentâs sporting interests and emphasises practical benefits for coaches and peers. The explicit ambition to âprovide a toolâ for training and the rationale for each chosen pre-activity show genuine curiosity and personal significance beyond simply completing an assignment.
Fourteen participants x five conditions x three trials yield rich data. The table is complete and units/uncertainty stated, satisfying âsufficient relevant raw dataâ.

The student planned five distinct treatment conditions, recruited 14 volunteers, prepared consent forms, created detailed warm-up scripts and collected 210 data points. Such initiative in design, implementation and presentation indicates strong personal input throughout the investigation.

Ethical consent forms, PAR-Q screening, clothing rules, warm-up injury prevention and area checks are all explicitly addressed, showing thorough awareness. Only minor omissions (e.g., data privacy storage) prevent full marks, so a high score within the top band is justified.

Subject terminology (e.g., standard deviation, coefficient of variation, plyometrics, plantar flexion) is used appropriately. Minor grammatical errors and inconsistent italicisation of variables are present but do not impede understanding.

The conclusion identifies running as most effective and references mean data and one significant t-test. Support is present but partial, as statistical insignificance of other differences is under-acknowledged.

annotations.comment: Safety and ethical considerations are thorough, including PAR-Q, consent, anonymity, and injury prevention, reflecting excellent ethical awareness.
Raw data table omits unit labels on table headers. Including âcmâ on each column heading ensures clear interpretation of measurement units.
The term âPloymetricsâ is misspelled in the raw data table. Correcting such errors enhances professionalism and prevents confusion.
Mean, standard deviation and coefficient of variation table uses excessive decimal places. Rounding to two significant figures improves readability.
Uncertainty is acknowledged only for measurement resolution. Expand discussion to include propagation of random error and trial variability for full uncertainty analysis.
Figure lacks axis labels and units, which hinders interpretation. Adding âHeight (cm)â to the vertical axis and clear legend will improve communication.
T-test results table presents p-values to nine decimal places. Reporting to three decimal places (e.g., p=0.049) aligns with standard reporting conventions.

The research question title lacks grammatical precision, notably the missing apostrophe in âgirlsâ. Revising to âgirlsâ vertical jumpâ and simplifying wording will improve clarity and conciseness.
Strong personal engagement is evident in the introduction through the candidateâs motivation to apply findings to sporting peers, reflecting independent thinking and real-world relevance.
Reliance on non-peer-reviewed sources such as YouTube and blogs weakens the academic rigor of the literature review. Incorporate more primary research or meta-analyses.
Biomechanical description is clear but could be strengthened with a diagram or more detailed explanation of muscle contraction kinetics to deepen the theoretical framework.
The hypotheses are stated but the null hypothesis wording is imprecise. Reframe Hâ as âno significant difference between mean jump heights across pre-activity conditionsâ.
The independent variables table repeats control descriptions inconsistently, leading to confusion. Standardize wording and consolidate control methods in a single column.
Controlled variables table systematically addresses confounds such as location and participant number, demonstrating thorough experimental design awareness.
Uncontrolled variables table header is blank, hindering readability. Add a descriptive column title such as âVariableâ to improve clarity.
The equipment list contains stray LaTeX symbols (e.g., \blacksquare) which distract from content. Cleaning these will enhance professional presentation.
Methodology section is replicable but lacks randomization or counterbalancing of session order to mitigate sequence effects. Consider a Latin square design.
The base warm-up is well detailed and consistently applied across sessions, demonstrating strong control of participant readiness and safety.
Pre-activity protocols table has misaligned columns for yoga and run sections. Revising cell spans and headers will clarify activity-duration mapping.
Discussion overstates practical significance for non-significant comparisons. Temper conclusions to reflect statistical insignificance for p>0.05.
The coefficient of variation discussion correctly defines CV but misses linking high CV to small sample reliability. Evaluate how data spread affects confidence.
Grammar error in conclusion paragraph (âHypothesis was not fully supportâ). Revising to âsupportedâ ensures professionalism.
Negative discussion cites âLonger legs mean less force productionâ without mechanistic explanation. Elaborate on force-length relationship or cite primary biomechanics source.
Interpretation of error bar overlap is unclear. Clarify that overlapping error bars suggest no significant difference, not absolute superiority.
Future improvements propose more homogeneous participants and extended training, but lack random assignment. Suggest matched-pairs or crossover design.
Personal engagement is maintained through the consistent reference to organizing sessions and creating warm-up scripts, demonstrating initiative and ownership.
Conclusion section is verbose and repeats prior discussion. Condense summary to key findings and implications to improve focus and reader engagement.
Bibliography entries show inconsistent formatting (varied date placement, missing italics). Adopt a single citation style (e.g., APA) for coherence.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: Justification shows little personal significance or curiosity
Little evidence of personal input in design, implementation or presentation
Limited evidence of personal engagement with little independent thinking
Clear evidence of personal input and initiative throughout the investigation
Clear evidence of personal engagement with significant independent thinking
Justification demonstrates clear personal significance and curiosity

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Evidence of personal engagement and independent thinking

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: Superficial or limited relevance background information
Research question stated but not focused
Methodology appropriate to very limited extent
Mainly appropriate and relevant background information
Relevant but not fully focused research question
Entirely appropriate and relevant background information
Highly appropriate methodology addressing all significant factors
Relevant and fully focused research question
Sufficient relevant raw data supporting detailed conclusion
Basic outline of practical issues only
Some relevant comparison to scientific context
Detailed, relevant and fully supported conclusion
Clear understanding of methodological issues demonstrated
Correct and justified comparison to scientific context
Many errors in terminology and conventions
Appropriate use of terminology with minimal errors
Well structured with all necessary information
